narrative:
Dear Student,

I understand that receiving a rejection for your university application can be disheartening, and I want to help you understand the reasons behind this decision. The model used to evaluate your application predicted a 66% probability that you might not pass the bar exam on your first attempt. This prediction led to the rejection of your application, as the university aims to admit students who are likely to succeed in passing the bar exam.

Let's look at the factors that influenced this prediction. Your academic performance, particularly your grades in Year 1 and Year 3, were significant contributors. You were placed in the lower deciles compared to your peers, which means your grades were below average. This pushed the prediction towards a higher likelihood of failing the bar exam. Additionally, your LSAT score was lower than the average, which also contributed to the prediction of failure.

Your family income bracket was considered as well, and while it was not as influential as your academic performance, it still played a role. Your undergraduate GPA was another factor, and it was below the average, which again nudged the prediction towards failure. On the other hand, your racial background, being Asian, and your gender, being female, had a relatively minor influence on the prediction.

The most influential factors were your academic deciles and LSAT score, followed by family income and undergraduate GPA. These factors collectively pushed the prediction towards a higher chance of failing the bar exam. However, it's important to note that your full-time status and gender had minimal impact on the decision.

Moving forward, you might consider focusing on improving your academic performance and LSAT score, as these are areas where you can make significant changes. Enhancing your grades and test scores could improve your chances of passing the bar exam and strengthen your future applications. Remember, this prediction is not a definitive measure of your potential, and with dedication and effort, you can work towards achieving your goals.

I hope this explanation helps you understand the decision and guides you on your path forward. If you have any further questions or need support, please feel free to reach out.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
